verdicts.note: The provided URL https://pathlit.mintlify.app/docs/nodes/pitchbook-agent points to a 'node' or 'agent' in a third-party platform (Pathlit), not an official MCP server. The evidence does mention MCP (Writer MCP gateway) but only as infrastructure for Writer's connector, not as a standalone PitchBook MCP server. No dedicated MCP server for PitchBook is evidenced.
The URL provided is not evidenced in the EVIDENCE. It appears to be a third-party integration doc, not a dedicated MCP server repository or endpoint.
Evidence confirms API key authentication with header format 'Authorization: PB-Token {API_KEY}' and that keys are issued by account managers, not self-generated.
Evidence confirms no self-serve signup; must contact PitchBook account manager/support for API access and key generation. This aligns with 'partnership-contact-sales'.
Evidence confirms REST API with base URL https://api.pitchbook.com and lists endpoints for companies, deals, people, investors, funds, and account/credits. Breadth of 'medium' is reasonable.
Evidence supports 'buildable-with-caveats' since the API is functional but requires enterprise contract and manual provisioning, limiting self-serve onboarding.
Evidence confirms that API access requires contacting account manager, purchasing credits/contract, and manual key provisioning—no self-serve key generation.
The one-liner accurately describes the REST API, authentication method, and enterprise-only access model as confirmed by the evidence.

revised.name: PitchBook
revised.one_liner: PitchBook offers a REST API (companies, deals, people, investors, funds) authenticated via an API key issued by an account manager, but access is enterprise-only with no self-serve signup.

auth_methods.notes: Header format: Authorization: PB-Token {API_KEY}. Key issued by account manager, not self-generated via a dashboard signup flow.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier or self-serve API signup; must contact a PitchBook account manager, purchase a custom enterprise contract, and receive credits/API key. Each request consumes purchased credits.

api_surface.notes: Base URL https://api.pitchbook.com with endpoints for companies, companies/search, deals, deals/search, people, people/search, investors, funds, and account/credits.

revised.main_blocker: API access requires a paid enterprise contract and manual provisioning by a PitchBook account manager; no self-serve key generation, which limits automated onboarding for an agent toolkit.

evidence.supports: Describes REST API v2, base URL, endpoints, PB-Token auth header, credit-based usage, and manual key provisioning via account manager.
Confirms API key authentication and that access requires contacting PitchBook support/account for API key generation.
Indicates no free tier and enterprise-level custom pricing for PitchBook.
